body:
Specifies the network protocol for streaming live telemetry from driver PC (LMUPI) to engineer PCs (TeleMU).

### Protocol design

- **UDP data channel** for telemetry frames — sequence numbers for loss detection, no retransmission
- **TCP control channel** for handshake, channel map exchange, heartbeat/keepalive
- **LZ4 block-mode compression** per-packet, falls back to raw when compression doesn't help

### Packet formats

Binary wire formats defined for both channels:

- **TCP control header**: version (`uint16`), message type, flags, length-prefixed payload
- **UDP telemetry header** (20 bytes): version, flags, channel count, sequence number (`uint32`), timestamp (`uint64` µs), compressed payload
- Max UDP payload **1352 bytes** (1400 MTU − IP/UDP/telemetry headers), with documented fragmentation via flags bits

### Control protocol

- 8 message types: `HELLO`, `SUBSCRIBE`, `CHANNEL_MAP`, `CONFIG_ACK`, `HEARTBEAT`/`ACK`, `DISCONNECT`, `ERROR`
- Channel map entries carry ID, name, data type, unit, sample rate
- Heartbeat every 2s, 5s timeout, 3 missed → disconnect

### Multi-client

- 1:N architecture (one driver, many engineers), independent subscriptions and sequence counters
- Configurable max clients (default 8), capacity rejection via error code `0x0003`

### Files changed

- `docs/docs/architecture/streaming-protocol.md` — new protocol spec with Mermaid diagrams
- `docs/mkdocs.yml` — added nav entry under Architecture

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Define telemetry streaming protocol specification</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Description
> Design the network protocol for streaming telemetry from driver PC to engineer PC(s).
> 
> ## Requirements
> - UDP for telemetry data (low latency, acceptable packet loss)
> - TCP control channel for handshake, config, and reliability
> - Protocol: version, channel map exchange, compressed data frames
> - Packet format: sequence number, timestamp, compressed payload
> - Support multiple concurrent clients (1 driver → N engineers)
> - Heartbeat/keepalive mechanism
> - Document protocol in `docs/`
> 
> ## Design Considerations
> - UDP packet size < 1400 bytes to avoid fragmentation
> - LZ4 per-packet compression
> - Sequence numbers for loss detection (not retransmission)</issue_description>
>

commits.messageBody:
Define the network protocol for streaming telemetry from driver PC
to engineer PCs, covering UDP data channel, TCP control channel,
packet formats, LZ4 compression, heartbeat, and multi-client support.

Co-authored-by: SeamusMullan <43112447+SeamusMullan@users.noreply.github.com>
